Listen to 2 HRT news items.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
What is the instrumental of ljubav?
Review job vocabulary.

activities.title: Instrumental in -ju, and the dob / doba trap
activities.intro: Two small but exam-favourite items. Consonant-final feminine nouns fuse the instrumental into -ju, triggering sound changes (sol → solju, ljubav → ljubavlju, radost → radošću). And two look-alikes must never be confused: dob (age) is feminine, doba (period, season) is neuter and never changes in the singular.

items.en: word → with a word
items.note: i-declension feminine. The fused -ju form: reci to jednom riječju = say it in a single word.

items.en: night → at / by night
items.note: Radim noću, a spavam danju. noću works as an adverb: 'at night'.

items.en: love → with love
items.note: Sound change v → vlj at the boundary: Kuha s ljubavlju = She cooks with love.

items.en: salt → with salt
items.note: l + j: Posoli jelo solju = Season the dish with salt.

items.en: blood → with blood
items.note: Same v → vlj: povezani krvlju = bound by blood.

items.en: joy → with joy
items.note: st → šć: Primili su nas s radošću = They welcomed us with joy.

items.en: thought → with a thought
items.note: sao → šlj: Bori se s crnom mišlju. (mixed with an adjective, -i is also possible: s tom mišlju/misli.)

items.en: form depends on a modifier
items.note: Alone, -ju is preferred (s ljubavlju); with an adjective, -i is common (s velikom ljubavi).

items.en: age (feminine, i-declension)
items.note: u mojoj dobi = at my age (locative dobi); srednja dob = middle age. Do NOT confuse with doba.

items.en: period, era, season (neuter, unchanged in sg.)
items.note: godišnje doba = season; u to doba = at that time. Same form in every singular case.

items.en: Children of that age love cartoons.
items.note: dob genitive dobi (te dobi = of that age); the collective djeca takes a plural verb (vole).

items.hr: Proljeće je moje najdraže godišnje doba.
items.en: Spring is my favourite season.
items.note: godišnje doba stays put; only the adjective najdraže changes to agree.

activities.title: Fused instrumentals and dob / doba
activities.intro: Build the -ju instrumentals with their sound changes, then keep dob (age) and doba (season) firmly apart — a classic exam gap-fill trap.

questions.prompt: Which noun means 'age'?
questions.explanation: dob (f.) = age (u mojoj dobi). doba (n.) = a period/season (godišnje doba).

questions.prompt: Instrumental with sound change: Posoli jelo ______. (sol)
questions.explanation: sol → solju (l + j) = with salt. The fused -ju instrumental.

questions.prompt: Instrumental: Kuha s ______. (ljubav)
questions.explanation: ljubav → ljubavlju (v → vlj) = with love.

questions.prompt: Complete: Primili su nas s ______.
questions.explanation: radost → radošću (st → šć). Standing without an adjective, the fused -ju (→ šću) form is preferred.

questions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'doba' correctly?
options: U mojoj dobi ima puno posla.
questions.explanation: doba is neuter and invariable: godišnje doba (season). 'U mojoj dobi' would need dob (age), not doba.

questions.prompt: Complete the locative: U mojoj ______ ljudi obično već rade. (dob)
questions.explanation: dob (f., i-declension) → locative dobi: u mojoj dobi = at my age.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I work at night and sleep during the day.'
questions.explanation: noću (instrumental of noć) and danju work as adverbs 'at night' / 'by day'; a = 'and (by contrast)'.

questions.prompt: Complete: Bori se s crnom ______. (misao)
questions.explanation: misao → mišlju (sao → šlj). With the adjective 'crnom', the plain -i (crnom misli) is also acceptable, but mišlju is offered here.

questions.prompt: Complete: Djeca te ______ vole crtiće. (dob, genitive)
questions.explanation: 'children of that age' = djeca te dobi; dob → genitive dobi (i-declension, like the locative).

days.title: Week 25 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate the work theme; hold a long conversation.
days.paretoFocus: You now own the exam's CV genre. Keep it warm while the review locks in verbal nouns.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): work, careers, the Croatian job market.
Record 2 minutes: your work history, from memory; listen back.
Cold-test the raditi family and 5 verbal nouns.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Form riječju-type instrumentals for 3 nouns.

activities.title: The world of work: week 25 consolidation
activities.intro: Everything from this week in one place, ready for tonight's long conversation: the raditi family, verbal nouns, job-ad vocabulary and CV frames, all worked into sentences you can actually say about careers and the Croatian job market.

items.en: Youth employment is a big problem.
items.note: The verbal noun zapošljavanje as the subject; mladih = genitive plural (of young people).

items.hr: Zaradio sam dovoljno za godišnji odmor.
items.en: I earned enough for a holiday.
items.note: raditi family: zaraditi; za + accusative (za godišnji odmor).

items.hr: Prijavio sam se na tri natječaja.
items.en: I applied to three vacancies.
items.note: prijaviti se na + accusative; tri natječaja = genitive singular after 'three'.

items.hr: Radim na neodređeno i zadovoljan sam.
items.en: I have a permanent contract and I'm satisfied.
items.note: raditi na neodređeno = to work on a permanent contract; zadovoljan (m.) = satisfied.

items.hr: Za to se radno mjesto traži znanje jezika.
items.en: That position requires knowledge of a language.
items.note: A se-construction (traži se); znanje = verbal noun + genitive jezika.

items.hr: Obrazovanje i iskustvo njegove su prednosti.
items.en: Education and experience are his strengths.
items.note: obrazovanje = verbal noun; prednost = strength (interview vocabulary of day 176).

items.hr: Nakon diplome počeo sam raditi u struci.
items.en: After graduation I started working in my field.
items.note: nakon + genitive (nakon diplome); struka = one's profession/field.

items.en: The boss offered me a promotion.
items.note: dative mi; napredovanje = advancement/promotion (another -nje noun).

items.en: I'm thinking about changing jobs.
items.note: razmišljati o + locative (o promjeni); promjena posla = a job change.

items.en: The employer offers flexible working hours.
items.note: poslodavac (day 174); radno vrijeme = working hours.

items.hr: U slobodno se vrijeme bavim volontiranjem.
items.en: In my free time I do volunteering.
items.note: baviti se + instrumental; volontiranje = volunteering, one more verbal noun.

items.hr: Da imam bolju plaću, ostao bih na ovom poslu.
items.en: If I had a better salary, I'd stay in this job.
items.note: A conditional preview of week 28: da + present, then ostao bih.

activities.title: Work theme: mixed review
activities.intro: Sweep the week: verbal nouns, the raditi family, job vocabulary and the cases that hold them together — with one listening pass from your wife.

questions.prompt: What does 'napredovanje' mean?
questions.explanation: napredovati → napredovanje = advancement / promotion at work.

questions.prompt: Form the verbal noun: zapošljavati → ______
questions.explanation: zapošljavati → zapošljavanje = the process of hiring / employment.

questions.prompt: Complete the raditi family: ______ sam dovoljno za odmor.
questions.explanation: za- = earn: Zaradio sam = I earned. odraditi = get a shift done, preraditi = rework, obraditi = process.

questions.prompt: Listen to your wife. What news does she share about her work?
options: her boss offered her a promotion
questions.answer: her boss offered her a promotion
questions.explanation: Šef mi je danas ponudio napredovanje = my boss offered me a promotion today.
questions.audioText: Imam dobru vijest! Šef mi je danas ponudio napredovanje. Ako pristanem, radit ću na neodređeno i imat ću veću plaću.

questions.prompt: Complete: Prijavio sam se ______ tri natječaja. ('to')
questions.explanation: prijaviti se na + accusative: na tri natječaja = to three vacancies.

questions.prompt: Complete the se-construction: Za to radno mjesto ______ znanje jezika.
questions.explanation: Impersonal se-construction: traži se znanje = knowledge is required. The subject is znanje (3rd sg.).

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'After graduation I started working in my field.'
questions.answer: Nakon diplome počeo sam raditi u struci
questions.explanation: nakon + genitive (Nakon diplome); počeo sam raditi; u + locative struci.

questions.prompt: Complete: Razmišljam ______ promjeni posla.
questions.explanation: razmišljati o + locative: o promjeni posla = about a job change.

questions.prompt: Complete the conditional (male speaker): Da imam bolju plaću, ______ na ovom poslu. (ostati)
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: da + present, then kondicional ostao bih (m. sg.) = I would stay.

activities.title: Večernji razgovor: posao i karijera
activities.intro: Tonight's long conversation is about work, careers and the Croatian job market. Read your lines ('Me') aloud — masculine participles, the week's vocabulary put to real use.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Kako je bilo danas na poslu? Izgledaš zamišljeno.
lines.en: How was work today? You look pensive.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Dobro, ali razmišljam o promjeni posla. Prijavio sam se na dva natječaja.
lines.en: Good, but I'm thinking about changing jobs. I applied to two vacancies.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Stvarno? A što ti se ne sviđa na ovom poslu?
lines.en: Really? And what don't you like about this job?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Sve je u redu, ali nema napredovanja. Radim isto već tri godine.
lines.en: Everything's fine, but there's no advancement. I've been doing the same thing for three years.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Razumijem. Kakvi su uvjeti u tim novim oglasima?
lines.en: I understand. What are the conditions in those new ads?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Jedan nudi ugovor na neodređeno i veću plaću. Traži se znanje jezika, a to imam.
lines.en: One offers a permanent contract and a higher salary. Knowledge of a language is required, and I have that.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: To zvuči odlično! A zapošljavanje je danas teško, pa dobro razmisli.
lines.en: That sounds great! But hiring is hard these days, so think it over carefully.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Znam. Zato sam već napisao novi životopis i zamolbu.
lines.en: I know. That's why I've already written a new CV and cover letter.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Da imaš intervju, kako bi objasnio zašto odlaziš?
lines.en: If you had an interview, how would you explain why you're leaving?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Rekao bih da tražim posao u kojem mogu napredovati i učiti nešto novo.
lines.en: I'd say I'm looking for a job in which I can advance and learn something new.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Savršen odgovor. Podržavam te — javi im se sutra.
lines.en: A perfect answer. I support you — get in touch with them tomorrow.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Hoću. A večeras slavimo tvoje napredovanje, ne moje brige!
lines.en: I will. And tonight we celebrate your promotion, not my worries!

days.title: Verb family: zvati + mock-exam briefing
days.objective: Master zvati (nazvati, pozvati, odazvati se, izazvati) and learn tomorrow's exam format.
days.paretoFocus: Tomorrow you sit your first full mock. Knowing the 5-section format (NN 100/2021) is half the battle.
drills: Words tab: verb-families pack — the zvati family.
One sentence per member: nazvati (phone), pozvati (invite), izazvati (provoke).
Read the mock-exam format in the Quiz tab: 5 sections, ≥60% on listening/reading/grammar, pass/fail writing & speaking.
Early night — treat tomorrow like the real thing.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name the exam's 5 sections from memory.
Contrast pozvati vs nazvati.

activities.title: The zvati family: call, invite, provoke
activities.intro: One last verb family before the mock. zvati (to call) takes na- to phone, po- to invite, iz- to provoke or cause, od- to respond. Learn the prefix, decode the verb — and never again mix up nazvati (phone) with pozvati (invite).

items.en: to call → I call…
items.note: The base, with the present stem zov-: Kako se zoveš? = What's your name?

items.en: to phone, to call up
items.note: na- = reach by phone. Nazvat ću te sutra. = I'll call you tomorrow.

items.en: to invite
items.note: po- here = invite. Pozvali su nas na večeru. = They invited us to dinner.

items.en: to respond (to a call / invitation)
items.note: od- = answer back; + dative: Odazvao sam se pozivu. = I responded to the invitation.

items.en: to provoke, to cause
items.note: iz- = bring out. Oluja je izazvala veliku štetu. = The storm caused great damage.

items.en: to convene, to call together
items.note: sa- = together. Sazvali su hitan sastanak. = They called an emergency meeting.

items.en: call; invitation (noun)
items.note: The noun behind the family: propušten poziv = missed call; poslati poziv = to send an invitation.

items.hr: Nazvao sam ga, ali se nije javio.
items.en: I called him, but he didn't answer.
items.note: nazvati koga (accusative); javiti se = to answer / pick up. Two everyday verbs together.

items.en: invite vs phone
items.note: Pozvao sam prijatelje = I invited friends. Nazvao sam prijatelje = I phoned friends. Different prefix, different act.

items.hr: Izazov je velik, ali prihvaćam ga.
items.en: The challenge is big, but I accept it.
items.note: izazov = challenge (from izazvati) — the noun you'll want when you talk about the exam.

activities.title: Exam briefing: the five sections
activities.intro: Tomorrow you sit your first full mock. Knowing the shape of the exam is half the battle. These are the five sections in official order, the scoring rule, and the conditions you should simulate — straight from the format the app's B1 mock follows.

items.en: I. Listening comprehension
items.note: Each recording plays twice; the text stays hidden. Read the questions first, then listen.

items.en: II. Reading comprehension
items.note: Genres: a biography, an ad, an interview, plus a word-bank gap-fill. The answer is always in the text.

items.en: III. Grammar structures
items.note: Choose the correct form. Dijakritici se broje — č, ć, š, ž, đ count as errors if wrong.

items.en: IV. Writing
items.note: Two tasks of 7-8 sentences, e.g. describe a picture and write your životopis (CV).

items.en: V. Speaking
items.note: A conversation with the examiner: introduce yourself, retell an event, give opinions.

items.en: Pass mark: at least 60%
items.note: You need ≥60% in each of listening, reading and grammar.

items.hr: Pisanje i govorenje: prolaz ili pad
items.en: Writing and speaking: pass or fail
items.note: These two are judged pass/fail against a rubric; every section must be passed.

items.en: No dictionary and no pausing.
items.note: Simulate real conditions: audio plays twice, but you don't stop it or look words up.

items.en: You hear each text twice.
items.note: Use the first pass for the gist, the second to confirm details — a key listening tactic.

items.en: challenge
items.note: Treat tomorrow as an izazov, not a verdict: it maps what stands between you and B1.

activities.title: zvati family + exam format
activities.intro: Separate the zvati prefixes, then lock down the facts of tomorrow's exam so nothing about the format can surprise you.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to phone someone'?
questions.explanation: na- + zvati = to reach by phone: Nazvat ću te. pozvati = invite, izazvati = provoke, sazvati = convene.

questions.prompt: 'They invited us to dinner.' — ______ su nas na večeru.
questions.explanation: pozvati = to invite: Pozvali su nas na večeru. Nazvali would mean 'they phoned us'.

questions.prompt: Complete the present: Ja te ______ svaki dan. (zvati)
questions.explanation: zvati has the present stem zov-: ja zovem, ti zoveš, on zove.

questions.prompt: 'The storm caused great damage.' — Oluja je ______ veliku štetu.
questions.explanation: izazvati = to cause / provoke: izazvati štetu = to cause damage.

questions.prompt: How many of the five sections require at least 60% to pass?
questions.explanation: ≥60% is required in listening, reading and grammar; writing and speaking are pass/fail.

questions.prompt: Complete: Odazvao sam se ______. (poziv, dative)
questions.explanation: odazvati se + dative: odazvati se pozivu = to respond to the invitation. poziv → pozivu.

questions.prompt: In the listening section, how many times do you hear each text?
options: as many times as you like
questions.explanation: Each recording plays exactly twice, like in the real exam — first for the gist, then for details.

questions.prompt: Put the words in order: 'I called him, but he didn't answer.'
questions.answer: Nazvao sam ga ali se nije javio
questions.explanation: Nazvao sam ga (perfekt + clitic ga), then the negative javiti se: nije se javio → ali se nije javio.

questions.prompt: Which is a real exam condition to simulate tomorrow?
options: no dictionary and no pausing the audio
you may use a dictionary for grammar
you translate everything into English first
questions.answer: no dictionary and no pausing the audio
questions.explanation: Simulate the real thing: bez rječnika, bez pauziranja; the audio plays twice and you don't stop it.

Self-grade writing against the model answers honestly.

activities.title: Before the mock: strategy for all five sections
activities.intro: Milestone day. This is your half-way check — a precise map of what stands between you and B1, not a final verdict. Read these ten tactics, then take the ten-question mixed mock below, and finally sit the full five-section exam in the Quiz tab.

items.en: Read all the questions before listening.
items.note: You'll know what to listen for on the very first pass.

items.hr: Slušanje: prvi put uhvati smisao, drugi put provjeri detalje.
items.en: Listening: first pass for the gist, second for details.
items.note: Each text plays twice — use the two passes differently.

items.hr: Čitanje: prvo pitanja, pa tekst; odgovor je u tekstu.
items.en: Reading: questions first, then the text; the answer is in the text.
items.note: Don't guess from outside knowledge — locate the exact sentence.

items.hr: Gramatika: pazi na dijakritike — č, ć, š, ž, đ se broje.
items.en: Grammar: mind the diacritics — č, ć, š, ž, đ count.
items.note: A missing dijakritik is a wrong answer. Slow down on spelling.

items.hr: Pisanje: drži se zadatka, 7-8 rečenica, provjeri padeže.
items.en: Writing: stick to the task, 7-8 sentences, check the cases.
items.note: Cover every point asked; re-read once for case and diacritic errors.

items.hr: Govorenje: govori povezano, ne stani na pola.
items.en: Speaking: speak in connected sentences, don't stop mid-way.
items.note: Use connectors (najprije, zatim, zato); self-correct rather than freeze.

items.en: Don't translate literally from English.
items.note: Trust Croatian patterns: sviđa mi se, not 'ja volim to' word-for-word.

items.hr: Ostani miran — ovo je polovišna provjera.
items.en: Stay calm — this is a half-way check.
items.note: You won't pass everything yet, and that is exactly the point.

items.hr: Puni ispit uzmi u kartici Kvizovi (B1 mock).
items.en: Take the full exam in the Quiz tab (B1 mock).
items.note: The complete five-section mock lives there — this LEARN + exercise only warms you up.

items.en: Note your percentage for each section.
items.note: Your weakest section gets extra attention this month; the ≥60% rule applies to each of the first three.

activities.title: Half-way mock: 10 mixed B1 questions
activities.intro: A compact sweep of everything B1-core so far — aspect in context, verb families, the passive, cases and the conditional — across the official section types: two listening items, a reading text, grammar gap-fills and one longer reorder. Then sit the full mock in the Quiz tab.

questions.prompt: [Listening] Koje radno vrijeme ima knjižnica radnim danom?
questions.explanation: The announcement says the library is open radnim danom od osam do dvadeset sati = weekdays 8:00-20:00.
questions.audioText: Poštovani korisnici, gradska knjižnica radi radnim danom od osam do dvadeset sati, a subotom od osam do trinaest sati. Nedjeljom ne radimo.

questions.prompt: [Listening] TOČNO ili NETOČNO: Knjižnica radi nedjeljom.
questions.explanation: The recording ends with 'Nedjeljom ne radimo' = we are closed on Sundays. So the statement is false.
questions.audioText: Poštovani korisnici, gradska knjižnica radi radnim danom od osam do dvadeset sati, a subotom od osam do trinaest sati. Nedjeljom ne radimo.

questions.prompt: Choose the verb-family verb: Liječnik me pažljivo ______ i objasnio mi je nalaze.
questions.explanation: pregledati = to examine (a patient); pogledati = take a look, ugledati = catch sight of, razgledati = sightsee.

questions.prompt: [Aspect] Jučer sam napokon ______ cijeli izvještaj. (napisati, male speaker)
questions.explanation: A completed whole → perfective napisati → napisao. 'pisao sam' would mean 'I was writing' (unfinished).

questions.prompt: [Reading] Pročitaj: 'Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić rođena je 1874. u Ogulinu. Pisala je priče za djecu i danas je najpoznatija hrvatska dječja književnica. Njezino najpoznatije djelo je Priče iz davnine.' — Gdje je rođena Ivana Brlić-Mažuranić?
questions.explanation: The text states 'rođena je 1874. u Ogulinu' — she was born in Ogulin. The answer is directly in the passage.

questions.prompt: [Case] Živim u ______ već pet godina. (Zagreb → locative)
questions.explanation: Static location after u → locative: Zagreb → u Zagrebu.

questions.prompt: [Passive] Complete: Taj je most ______ 1926. godine.
questions.explanation: sagraditi → sagrađen (d → đ); masculine most → sagrađen je = was built. (Never plain 'sagraden' or the perfekt 'sagradio' here.)

questions.prompt: [Conditional] Da imam više vremena, ______ ti. (pomoći, male speaker)
questions.explanation: Unreal condition: da + present, then kondicional pomogao bih (m. sg.) = I would help you.

questions.prompt: [Grammar] I mi ______ rado došli na proslavu!
questions.explanation: 1st person plural conditional is bismo (never colloquial 'bi' for all persons): I mi bismo rado došli.

questions.prompt: [Longer reorder] Put the words in order: 'If I had more time, I would read every day.'
questions.answer: Kad bih imao više vremena čitao bih svaki dan
questions.explanation: Potential condition: kad + kondicional in both clauses — Kad bih imao … čitao bih …; više vremena = genitive; svaki dan = accusative of time.

days.objective: Understand and form the present verbal adverb: čitajući, govoreći.
days.paretoFocus: ASOO B1 grammar. You mostly need to READ it (news style: 'Govoreći o krizi, premijer je rekao…') — production is a bonus.
drills: Form: 3rd pl. present + -ći: čitaju > čitajući, govore > govoreći.
Form the adverb of 8 verbs.
Find 2 examples in HRT articles (news style loves them).
Rewrite 3 sentences: 'Dok sam čitao, pio sam kavu' > 'Čitajući, pio sam kavu'.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Form the verbal adverb of pjevati and raditi.
Review the staviti family.

days.title: Ravnopravni ili ne? — equality discussion
days.objective: Discuss equality topics with polite, structured disagreement.
days.paretoFocus: ASOO's ravnopravnost block. Sensitive topics demand precise hedging language — the highest register of B1 speech.
drills: Learn 12 words: prava, jednakost, ravnopravnost, diskriminacija, poštovanje, prilika…
Speaking session: discuss equality at work with your tutor or spouse.
Practise hedges: čini mi se, rekao/rekla bih, možda griješim, ali…
Listen to a full HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the hedging phrases.
Review the verbal adverb forms.

days.title: Writing: opinion text on equality
days.objective: Write your strongest opinion text yet — 12 sentences, full structure.
days.paretoFocus: The final Phase 4 writing rep: everything you've learned (participles, se-forms, conditionals, connectors) in one text.
drills: Write 12 sentences: 'Jesu li svi u društvu ravnopravni?'
Checklist: 1 passive participle, 1 se-construction, 1 conditional, 1 collective, 3 connectors.
Proofread diacritics letter by letter.
Get feedback from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Read your text aloud.
Update the error log; check off fixed items.

days.title: Week 30 review + long conversation
days.objective: Consolidate all 11 verb families and this week's grammar.
days.paretoFocus: The verb-family project is complete. Today proves you can deploy them in free conversation.
drills: Clear ALL due words in the Words tab.
Long conversation (20+ min): society, fairness, work — deliberately using 8+ family verbs.
Record 2 minutes: 'Što se može promijeniti u društvu' (use se-forms); listen back.
Cold-test pustiti + staviti families.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Note 2 improvements from your recording.
Quiz yourself on one random verb from each of the 11 families.

days.title: Smijeh je lijek — humor + grammar sweep I
days.objective: Enjoy Croatian humor and start the full B1 grammar sweep.
days.paretoFocus: Understanding jokes is real comprehension. Meanwhile, a systematic sweep finds gaps before exam prep begins.
drills: Read/watch 3 simple Croatian jokes or a comedy clip; explain ONE to your spouse in Croatian.
Grammar sweep part 1: aspect pairs, passive participle, verbal nouns — 5 self-test sentences each.
Note anything shaky in your error log.
Listen to a full HRT bulletin.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell your favourite joke.
Re-drill your shakiest grammar point.

days.title: Phase 4 milestone: B1 core complete
days.objective: Finish the grammar sweep and certify your B1 core. Exam prep starts tomorrow.
days.paretoFocus: 65 days of B1 building are done: 11 verb families, all core grammar, native listening, three writing genres. Now we sharpen for the exam.
drills: Grammar sweep part 2: dative experiencers, conditionals, collectives, se-constructions — 5 self-test sentences each.
Take the B1 grammar quiz in the app.
Record a 3-minute talk: 'Moj put do hrvatskog' — compare with your day 157 recording.
Long conversation with your tutor or spouse to celebrate.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write your 3 weakest areas — exam prep will target them.
Read the exam-prep phase overview in the app.

days.title: Exam prep begins: format mastery + listening A
days.objective: Know the exam inside-out and drill short-sentence listening.
days.paretoFocus: 25 days to exam-ready. The format (NN 100/2021): 5 sections, ≥60% each on listening/reading/grammar, pass/fail writing & speaking. No surprises allowed.
drills: Study the mock exam in the Quiz tab: section order, task types, points.
Listening drill I.A: six short sentences (heard twice), open short answers — traffic-report style.
Listen to a full HRT bulletin; answer 'koliko/gdje/kada' about 3 stories.
Start a fresh exam-phase error log.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Name all 5 exam sections and the pass rule.
Re-answer your 2 missed listening items.

days.title: Listening B: DA/NE statements
days.objective: Master the longer-text listening task with true/false statements.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Listening B: one longer text (event announcement), heard twice, six DA/NE statements. Strategy: read statements BEFORE the audio starts.
drills: Do the DA/NE listening drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Strategy: first listen = gist, second listen = verify each statement.
Listen to an HRT event/culture announcement; invent 4 DA/NE statements and answer them.
Log misses in the error log.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall the two-listen strategy.
Retell today's announcement in 3 sentences.

days.title: Reading A: biography + open questions
days.objective: Answer open questions on a ~150-word biography quickly and precisely.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Reading A: biography text, seven open questions. Answers must come FROM the text — no creativity, full sentences, correct forms.
drills: Do the biography reading drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab (Marija Jurić Zagorka style).
Practise locating answers: scan for names, dates, places first.
Write answers as full sentences with correct case endings.
Read one more short biography online for speed.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the biography in 4 sentences.
Review 2 errors from the log.

days.title: Reading B: word-bank gap fill
days.objective: Insert 12 bank words into a text in their exact case forms.
days.paretoFocus: The densest exam task: 12 points, vocabulary + case forms together (travnju, očiju, superlatives). Diacritic-perfect answers only.
drills: Do the word-bank gap-fill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
For each gap: identify the needed case BEFORE choosing the word.
Re-do every miss and say WHY the correct form fits.
Add missed forms to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Decline mjesec and oko in the tricky forms.
Recall 3 gap-fill traps.

days.title: Grammar A: form choice
days.objective: Choose correct forms: genitive plural, futur I spelling, bismo, prepositions.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Grammar A samples: '4 i pol milijuna stanovnika', 'ići ćemo', 'bismo', 'među' + instrumental, 'o njemu', 'ljepše'. Drill these exact patterns.
drills: Do the grammar form-choice drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Review: genitive plural after numbers/quantities.
Review: futur I orthography (ići ćemo vs radit ćemo) and bih/bismo.
Log every miss with its rule.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write 'we would go' and 'we will go' correctly.
Say '5 million inhabitants' with correct genitive.

days.title: Grammar B: aspect & prefix verbs
days.objective: Choose the right prefixed/aspect verb in context — your verb families pay off.
days.paretoFocus: Exam Grammar B is exactly the ASOO 'glagoli u kontekstu' material: napisala vs zapisala vs opisala. You trained 11 families for this.
drills: Do the aspect/prefix-verb drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Words tab: verb-families pack — full cold review of all 11 families.
For 8 sentences, choose the family verb and explain the prefix's meaning.
Log any family that still wobbles.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
One sentence each: opisati, propustiti, podržati.
Recall the meaning of prefixes iz-, pre-, do-.

days.title: Writing under time: describe a picture
days.objective: Write the 7-8 sentence picture description in 20 minutes flat.
days.paretoFocus: Exam writing task 1: opis slike — people, actions, appearance, setting, what they discuss. Time pressure is the skill now.
drills: Set a 20-minute timer; do the picture-description task from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Self-check against the rubric: all checklist points covered? 7-8 sentences? Diacritics?
Rewrite your 2 weakest sentences.
Get one native check from your partner.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite the picture checklist from memory.
Note timing: did you finish with minutes to spare?

days.title: Writing under time: životopis
days.objective: Write your 7-8 sentence CV in 20 minutes, error-free.
days.paretoFocus: Exam writing task 2 is your own biography. You wrote it on day 175 — now it must flow out under pressure, perfectly spelled.
drills: 20-minute timer: write the životopis task from the mock exam in the Quiz tab, WITHOUT looking at your day-175 version.
Compare with your polished version; merge the best of both.
Have your Croatian spouse do a final read.
Memorize your 3 hardest spellings (dates, place names).

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your CV aloud from memory.
Check every diacritic in today's text once more.

days.title: Speaking simulation #1
days.objective: Run a full oral-exam simulation.
days.paretoFocus: The oral exam is live conversation: introduce yourself, answer questions, discuss everyday topics. Simulate the pressure now, not on exam day.
drills: Full speaking simulation with your tutor (or spouse playing strict examiner): intro, biography questions, opinion questions, a picture to describe aloud.
Use the speaking prompts from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Record it; self-grade with the rubric: fluency, grammar, vocabulary, pronunciation.
Note 3 hesitation points to smooth out.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Re-answer your worst question, better.
Prepare mentally: tomorrow is full mock exam #1.

days.title: FULL TIMED MOCK EXAM #1
days.objective: Sit the complete exam under real conditions.
days.paretoFocus: Everything at once, timed, no aids. Target: ≥60% on listening, reading and grammar + pass both writing and speaking.
drills: Take the full mock exam in the Quiz tab: all 5 sections, one sitting, timed.
Real conditions: audio plays twice only, no dictionary, no pausing.
Write both writing tasks by hand if possible (the real exam is on paper).
Record your speaking answers; grade everything honestly against the rubrics.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write down your % per section next to the day-180 scores.
Rest — analysis is tomorrow, not tonight.

days.title: Mock #1 error-log review
days.objective: Dissect mock #1 and pick your two target sections.
days.paretoFocus: The gap between mock #1 and mock #2 is where the exam is won. Every error gets a category, a rule and a re-test date.
drills: Classify every miss: vocabulary / case / aspect / listening / diacritics / time pressure.
Compare with day 180: celebrate the growth, circle what's stuck.
Choose the 2 weakest sections — days 227-234 target them.
Add all missed words/forms to your notes; ask your partner about anything unclear.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your 2 target sections and why.
Re-do the 3 hardest questions from mock #1.

days.title: Weak-section day: listening intensive
days.objective: Double dose of exam-style listening.
days.paretoFocus: Twice-heard audio rewards note-taking on the first pass and verification on the second. Build that reflex.
drills: Re-do both listening drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab (short sentences + DA/NE).
Full HRT bulletin: write one fact per story, numbers included.
Dictation: transcribe 3 sentences from HRT audio word-for-word.
Log listening errors: was it speed, vocabulary or numbers?

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say 3 large numbers you heard today.
Retell the bulletin's top story.

days.title: Weak-section day: reading intensive
days.objective: Both exam reading genres, back-to-back, under time.
days.paretoFocus: Reading is 19 of the 42 written points — the biggest scored section. Speed + case-form precision decide it.
drills: Timed: biography + open questions from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Timed: word-bank gap-fill from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Read one HRT article; summarize in 3 sentences.
Log every miss with the rule that fixes it.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recall 3 gap-fill case traps.
Review yesterday's listening notes.

days.title: Weak-section day: grammar re-drill
days.objective: Re-drill every grammar structure you've missed since day 216.
days.paretoFocus: Your error log knows exactly which structures leak points. Fix the leaks, not the whole ocean.
drills: Re-do every grammar item marked wrong in your error log.
Words tab: verb-families pack — target the 3 wobbliest families.
Write 6 sentences using your personal top-3 error structures correctly.
Do one fresh grammar drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
State your top-3 grammar rules in your own words.
Check off fixed errors in the log.

days.title: Writing polish: both genres back-to-back
days.objective: Both writing tasks in one 40-minute sitting — like the real exam.
days.paretoFocus: On exam day you write opis + životopis in one session. Train the stamina and the switch between genres.
drills: 40-minute timer: picture description THEN životopis, from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Self-check both against rubrics; count sentences (7-8 each).
Fix diacritics with a coloured pen — make errors visible.
One native check on the weaker text.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Which genre cost more time? Plan the split.
Rewrite your single weakest sentence.

days.title: Speaking simulation #2
days.objective: Second full oral simulation — smoother, calmer, longer answers.
days.paretoFocus: Examiners reward answers of 3+ sentences. Train expanding: statement + reason + example, every time.
drills: Full speaking simulation with your tutor or Croatian spouse using the mock exam speaking prompts in the Quiz tab.
Rule: every answer gets 3+ sentences (statement, reason, example).
Record and compare with day 224 — hear the difference.
Drill your 3 hesitation points from last time.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Expand 'Volim li Hrvatsku?' into 4 sentences, aloud.
Note 2 remaining rough spots.

days.title: Listening endurance day
days.objective: A full bulletin + a longer audio segment + exam drills in one day.
days.paretoFocus: Exam audio will feel easy if HRT feels normal. Overtrain the ear this week.
drills: Full HRT bulletin in the morning: one fact per story.
One longer HRT audio segment (10+ min) on a familiar theme in the afternoon.
Re-do the DA/NE listening drill in the mock exam in the Quiz tab — aim for 6/6.
Shadow-speak 5 sentences right after the anchor says them.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Retell the audio segment in 4 sentences.
Review any number you misheard today.

days.title: Reading speed day
days.objective: Two full exam reading tasks under a strict clock.
days.paretoFocus: Slow reading steals time from writing. Target: finish each reading task with 25% of its time to spare for checking.
drills: Timed re-run: both reading tasks from the mock exam in the Quiz tab.
Then: skim one HRT article in 2 minutes and answer who/what/when.
Practise the scan: names, dates and numbers jump out first.
Log timing per task.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Did you beat your day-228 times?
Review 3 vocabulary items that slowed you down.

days.title: Grammar final sweep
days.objective: A last full pass over the exam's grammar catalogue — strict diacritics.
days.paretoFocus: Tomorrow is mock #2. Tonight your grammar must be boring: genitive plurals, bismo, ćemo-spelling, prefixed verbs — automatic.
drills: Do both grammar drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab with strict-diacritics mode.
Speed round: 10 aspect choices from the verb families, 30 seconds each.
Re-check your error log: every item should be checked off or re-drilled today.
Early night — mock #2 tomorrow.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Write 'ići ćemo', 'radit ćemo', 'bismo išli' correctly.
Name the 5 exam sections and the pass rule one more time.

days.title: FULL TIMED MOCK EXAM #2
days.objective: The dress rehearsal: pass all 5 sections.
days.paretoFocus: This is the go/no-go signal. ≥60% on listening, reading and grammar + two passes = you're ready to book the real thing.
drills: Take the full mock exam in the Quiz tab: all 5 sections, timed, one sitting, real conditions.
Writing by hand; speaking recorded and rubric-graded.
No aids, audio twice only — exactly like exam day.
Note scores next to mock #1 and day 180.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Log your % per section.
Rest. Analysis tomorrow.

days.title: Mock #2 error-log review + readiness call
days.objective: Compare mock #2 vs mock #1 and confirm your readiness picture.
days.paretoFocus: Three data points (day 180, 225, 235) show your trajectory. Anything still under 60% gets the final four days.
drills: Full error review: classify, compare with mock #1, celebrate every fixed category.
Check the pass rule per section: ≥60% × 3 + writing pass + speaking pass?
Pick the ONE weakest remaining section for day 237.
Add final missed items to the Words tab.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
State your section scores from memory.
Write tomorrow's plan in one sentence.

days.title: Final targeting: your weakest section
days.objective: One focused day on whatever section scored lowest in mock #2.
days.paretoFocus: Generic review is over. Today is surgical: one section, its task types, its traps, until it clears 60% comfortably.
drills: Re-do that section's drills in the mock exam in the Quiz tab twice.
Re-read the error-log entries for that section; drill each rule.
If it's listening: extra HRT bulletin. If reading/grammar: extra timed text. If writing/speaking: one more timed rep.
Finish with one confidence rep of your STRONGEST section.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Did the weak section clear 60% today?
Review 3 items from the error log.

days.title: Writing final: clean copies
days.objective: One perfect, timed copy of each writing genre.
days.paretoFocus: Writing is pass/fail — one clean, complete, 7-8 sentence text per genre is all that's asked. Make 'clean' your default.
drills: Timed: one picture description + one životopis (mock exam tasks in the Quiz tab), by hand.
Proofread pass 1: verb endings and cases. Pass 2: every č/ć/š/ž/đ.
Final native check by your Croatian spouse.
File both as your reference 'exam-day templates'.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your životopis one last time.
Recall the picture-description checklist.

days.title: Speaking final + exam-day strategy
days.objective: Last speaking simulation plus a calm exam-day playbook.
days.paretoFocus: You know the content. Today locks in composure: strategies for twice-heard audio, answer order, and what to do when a word won't come.
drills: Final relaxed speaking simulation with your tutor or Croatian spouse — enjoy it.
Rehearse recovery phrases: 'Kako da to kažem…', 'Mislim, htio/htjela sam reći…'
Write your exam-day playbook: listening (notes on pass 1, verify on pass 2), reading (scan first), writing (draft, count sentences, proofread diacritics).
Light HRT listening only — no cramming.

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Recite your exam-day playbook.
Sleep well — tomorrow you certify readiness.

days.title: FINAL: readiness check + book the real exam
days.objective: Confirm exam readiness in the app and book the official B1 exam.
days.paretoFocus: 240 days from the alphabet to a B1 booking. The certificate (NN 100/2021) is the legal proof of language for long-term residence and the citizenship pathway.
drills: Open the exam-readiness view in Progress: confirm all 5 sections passed and tick the can-do checklist.
Book the real exam: Croaticum (Zagreb), CEHAS or the Rijeka School of Croatian Studies — contact them about 10 days ahead and register with your ID.
Check practicalities: date, fee, documents, location.
Celebrate in Croatian with your spouse — you earned it: Uspio/Uspjela sam!

items: Clear your due words in the Words tab.
Say, in Croatian, what you'll do after passing the exam.
Keep the daily HRT habit until exam day — the ear must stay warm.
